Off Calendar Jan-28-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi for Scott Hodges (Administrator).

Ms. Alavi stated the terms of the sale for the record. The sale was confirmed at $724,000.00. An additional bond in the amount of $460,000.00 is required. The Court granted the petition.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy M. Svahlin.

Petition To Establish Ownership Of Real Property
Off Calendar Oct-22-2008 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number 290777 (The Ying Soo Chung Trust).

Appearances: Carol Greenberg for Margie Chung. Edward Koplowitz litigation counsel for Scott Hodges.

Mr. Koplowitz informed the Court that the case settled and that the parties agreed to 1/4 interest in the Broadway Steet property. The Court granted the petition, (subject to review of (2) orders). The Petition To Establish Ownership Of Real Property and the Petition To Determine Existence Of The Trust were taken off calendar.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.

Petition To Establish Ownership Of Real Property
Off Calendar Jul-09-2008 Continued To Sep-17-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case #290777 (The Ying Soo Chung Trust).

Appearances: Ed Koplowitz for Scott Hodges. Carol Greenberg for Margie Chung.

Mr. Koplowitz requested a continuance stating that the parties conducted discovery, that mediation was set for late August or September and that a stipulation consolidating the matters had been submitted. The matters were continued to September 17, 2008 for status.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Karen Farnsworth CSR#5162. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M.Loos.

Petition To Establish Ownership Of Real Property
Off Calendar Apr-16-2008 Continued To Jul-09-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The matter was called with case number 290777 (The Ying Soo Chung Trust).

Appearances: Edward Koplowitz for Scott Hodges (Administrator). Carol Greenberg for Margie Chung. Nancy Rasch for the Minors.

Mr. Koplowitz stated that discovery was still outstanding, that the response to the petition was at issue, that both matters should be consolidated for all purposes and suggested the possibilty of mediation in the future. Ms. Greenberg stated that she was willing to go to mediation, but that she wanted time to conduct discovery. The Court will grant the request to consolidate the matters for all purposes pursuant to stipulation. Mr. Koplowitz will submit the stipulation and order. The matter was continued to July 9, 2008 for status on discovery.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/ S. Ravinantapricha. Bailiff: Deputy J. Garrido.
